Summary: [FR] RVC-REQ-001 Power button start and stop
Description: Covers FR-001, FR-002, FR-003. The controller starts from power off. When the user presses the button while off, it powers on and starts the cleaning flow. When the user presses the button while on, it stops the motor, turns off the cleaner, and ends cleaning.

Covers FR-004, FR-005, FR-018, FR-019, FR-020. When powered on, the controller checks the front obstacle sensor. If the front path is clear, the motor moves forward and the cleaner turns on. The controller outputs defined motor and cleaner commands only.

Covers FR-006 through FR-014. When a front obstacle is detected, the controller turns the cleaner off and starts obstacle avoidance. It checks the left side first, then the right side. If both sides are blocked, it moves backward until a side becomes clear, turns toward the clear side, then resumes forward cleaning.

Summary: [FR] RVC-REQ-004 Dust detection and cleaner power-up
Description: Covers FR-015, FR-016, FR-017. When dust is detected during forward movement, the cleaner power increases for 5 seconds and then returns to normal. If a front obstacle is detected during power-up, the controller cancels the power-up timing, turns off the cleaner, and starts obstacle avoidance.

Covers NFR-004, NFR-005, NFR-006. Controller logic must be separated from hardware details and testable with GTest. GitHub Actions must run CMake build, GTest, Cppcheck, clang-tidy, SonarCloud analysis, and coverage collection.
